Typical range for industrial cleaning of aluminum to remove contaminants without melting; starting at 100W balances efficiency and safety. Max limited by aluminum's high reflectivity and thermal conductivity to avoid substrate damage. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on metal cleaning thresholds.

Effective speeds for light to heavy contamination on aluminum; slower for heavy (e.g., oxide layers), faster for light oils. Recommended 1500 mm/s optimizes removal while minimizing heat buildup due to aluminum's conductivity. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) case studies.

Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective industrial cleaning; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ but increases cost. 50 ns recommended for aluminum to ablate contaminants with minimal thermal diffusion. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) comparing pulse regimes for metals.

Typical for pulsed fiber lasers at 1064 nm; higher rates speed up cleaning but risk heat accumulation on reflective aluminum. 50 kHz balances speed and safety. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021) on frequency effects for aluminum surface preparation.

Fluence range for contaminant removal; aluminum damage threshold ~4-6 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² ensures safe cleaning below ablation threshold of substrate. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Physical Review B (2017) and Optics Express (2022) for IR laser on aluminum.

Beam diameter range for uniform coverage; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. 300 μm recommended for aluminum to achieve even fluence distribution without hotspots. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaners, with optimization in Surface and Coatings Technology (2019).

Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; aluminum's reflectivity often requires 2 passes for complete removal without damage. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Journal of Laser Applications (2020) on industrial aluminum cleaning.

Ensures uniform coverage and prevents gaps in cleaning; 70% recommended for aluminum to account for beam divergence and material reflectivity. Source: Process optimization studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2018).
